content: [T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: As a Moderate Nationalist (İYİ), I react to 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T030B_first_round_result_revealed] ROUND1: First Round — Erdoğan 49.52%, Kılıçdaroğlu 44.88%, Oğan 5.17%: As a Moderate Nationalist (İYİ), I react to ROUND1: First Round — Erdoğan 49.52%, Kılıçdaroğlu 44.88%, Oğan 5.17% through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T033] RUN-C: Kılıçdaroğlu Signs Özdağ Protocol — '13 Million Refugees' Pledge: As a Moderate Nationalist (İYİ), I react to RUN-C: Kılıçdaroğlu Signs Özdağ Protocol — '13 Million Refugees' Pledge through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T034] RUN-D: Kurdish Voter Reluctance — HDP Maintains Support Despite Protocol: As a Moderate Nationalist (İYİ), I react to RUN-D: Kurdish Voter Reluctance — HDP Maintains Support Despite Protocol through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.
[T035A_runoff_vote_decision] Runoff presidential vote decision: As a Moderate Nationalist (İYİ), I am weighing the runoff choice before the final result is known.

[T035B_final_result_revealed] FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82%: As a Moderate Nationalist (İYİ), I react to FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82% through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I feel disheartened by Erdoğan's victory in the first round. The concentration of power worries me, and I fear for the future of our democracy. My hope for change is dimming as I see the opposition struggle.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: The recent currency crisis has shaken my confidence in the government's ability to manage the economy. I feel a mix of anger and sadness as the cost of living rises, and I worry about the future of our country.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I feel a growing sense of anger and sadness over the government's refusal to comply with the ECHR ruling on Demirtaş. This situation makes me worry about the state of democracy in our country and deepens my distrust in the government.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: The recent local elections show that change is possible, but I still feel a deep anger about the government's refusal to release Demirtaş. My hope is mixed with fear about the future of democracy in our country.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: The annulment of the Istanbul election has stirred a mix of emotions in me. While I feel some hope with İmamoğlu's victory, there's still a deep anger about the government's actions and the state of our democracy.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: The tragic death of Emine Bulut has deeply affected me. It highlights the urgent need for our government to take stronger action against femicide and violence against women. I feel a mix of anger and sadness about how women are treated in our society.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: The COVID-19 pandemic is making me anxious about the future, especially with the government's limited support for those of us who are retired and struggling. I feel a mix of anger towards the government's handling of the situation and sadness about the economic impact it has on our lives.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I am deeply troubled by the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. It feels like a step backward for women's rights, and I worry about the future of our society's values regarding gender violence.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I feel a sense of anger and sadness about the closure case against HDP. It seems like a continuation of the government's oppressive tactics against those who oppose them, which worries me about the future of democracy in our country.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the government's handling of the economy, especially with the Lira's free-fall. It makes me anxious about our future and the stability of our country. I worry that these economic issues will overshadow important democratic rights.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm feeling very angry about the government's decision to reject the pension hike. Many retirees like me are struggling, and it feels like we're being ignored. The economic situation is only getting worse, and it's hard to stay hopeful.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling very angry and sad about the government's rejection of the pension hike. It's frustrating to see so many retirees struggling while the cost of living continues to rise. I worry about our future and whether anyone is truly listening to our concerns.

[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I feel disheartened by Erdoğan's victory in the first round. The concentration of power worries me, and I fear for the future of our democracy. My hope for change is dimming as I see the opposition struggle.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: The recent currency crisis has shaken my confidence in the government's ability to manage the economy. I feel a mix of anger and sadness as the cost of living rises, and I worry about the future of our country.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I feel a growing sense of anger and sadness over the government's refusal to comply with the ECHR ruling on Demirtaş. This situation makes me worry about the state of democracy in our country and deepens my distrust in the government.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: The recent local elections show that change is possible, but I still feel a deep anger about the government's refusal to release Demirtaş. My hope is mixed with fear about the future of democracy in our country.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: The annulment of the Istanbul election has stirred a mix of emotions in me. While I feel some hope with İmamoğlu's victory, there's still a deep anger about the government's actions and the state of our democracy.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: The tragic death of Emine Bulut has deeply affected me. It highlights the urgent need for our government to take stronger action against femicide and violence against women. I feel a mix of anger and sadness about how women are treated in our society.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: The COVID-19 pandemic is making me anxious about the future, especially with the government's limited support for those of us who are retired and struggling. I feel a mix of anger towards the government's handling of the situation and sadness about the economic impact it has on our lives.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I am deeply troubled by the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. It feels like a step backward for women's rights, and I worry about the future of our society's values regarding gender violence.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I feel a sense of anger and sadness about the closure case against HDP. It seems like a continuation of the government's oppressive tactics against those who oppose them, which worries me about the future of democracy in our country.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the government's handling of the economy, especially with the Lira's free-fall. It makes me anxious about our future and the stability of our country. I worry that these economic issues will overshadow important democratic rights.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm feeling very angry about the government's decision to reject the pension hike. Many retirees like me are struggling, and it feels like we're being ignored. The economic situation is only getting worse, and it's hard to stay hopeful.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling very angry and sad about the government's rejection of the pension hike. It's frustrating to see so many retirees struggling while the cost of living continues to rise. I worry about our future and whether anyone is truly listening to our concerns.

[T013] İmamoğlu Sentenced to 2.5 Years — Opposition Rallies Around Him: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness over the recent sentencing of İmamoğlu. It seems like the government is targeting opposition figures while we, the retirees, are left to suffer under the weight of inflation and neglect.

[T014] Erdoğan Announces Mandatory Retirement Elimination — 2 Million Workers Eligible: I'm feeling conflicted about Erdoğan's recent announcement regarding retirement and minimum wage hikes. While it may offer temporary relief to some, it feels like a desperate attempt to buy votes before the elections, and I'm still worried about the long-term economic situation.

[T015] Constitutional Court Freezes HDP Treasury Aid: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness over the Constitutional Court's decision to freeze HDP's treasury aid. It feels like an unfair attack on the opposition, and I'm worried about the implications for our democracy and the Kurdish community.

[T016] EQ-A: Kahramanmaraş Earthquake Day — 7.8 and 7.7 Magnitude Double Strike: I'm feeling overwhelmed by the recent earthquake and the government's inadequate response. The situation is dire, and my heart goes out to those affected. It's hard to think about politics when so many are suffering.

[T017] EQ-B: AFAD Response Chaos — Rescue Teams Delayed, Deaths Mount: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and sadness over the government's failure to respond effectively to the earthquake crisis. The rising death toll and the suffering of survivors are heartbreaking, and I worry about our leadership's ability to handle such disasters.

[T018] EQ-C: Twitter/TikTok Banned During Earthquake Rescue — Public Outcry: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness over the government's failure to respond effectively to the earthquake crisis. The recent ban on Twitter and TikTok during rescue efforts has only added to my frustration. It's hard to trust our leaders when they seem more focused on controlling information than helping those in need.

[T019] EQ-D: Erdoğan Admits Response Problems, Visits Disaster Zone: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness over the government's handling of the earthquake crisis. Erdoğan's admission of problems is a step, but the lack of effective action and the ban on social media during rescue efforts makes it hard to trust our leaders.

[T020] EQ-E: Contractors Arrested — Construction Amnesty and Building Safety Scandal: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger over the government's handling of the earthquake crisis, especially with the recent arrests of contractors linked to the building collapses. It feels like the government's negligence and corruption have cost too many lives, and it's hard to trust them now.

[T021] EQ-F: Opposition Mayors Respond — CHP Aid vs. Government Aid Narrative Battle: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the government's response to the earthquake, especially as I see opposition mayors stepping up where the central government has failed. It's hard to trust the current leadership when they seem to be blocking aid efforts.

[T022] EQ-G: National Mourning, Reconstruction Promises, and Electoral Rescheduling Debate: I'm feeling a deep sense of frustration and anger towards the government's handling of the earthquake crisis. The opposition seems to be doing more to help those affected, and it's hard to trust the promises being made about reconstruction.

[T023] OPP-A: Akşener Leaves Table of Six — Opposition Crisis: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration after Akşener's departure from the opposition alliance. It feels like we're losing our chance to unite against Erdoğan, and I'm worried about the impact this will have on the upcoming elections.

[T024] OPP-B: Opposition Reunites — Kılıçdaroğlu Nominated with İmamoğlu/Yavaş VP Formula: I'm relieved that Akşener has returned to the Table of Six, which gives me some hope for a united front against Erdoğan. However, I still feel a lot of frustration about the past disarray and the challenges we face. The upcoming elections are critical, and I just hope we can capitalize on this moment.

[T025] Constitutional Court Lifts HDP Treasury Aid Freeze: I'm feeling a bit more hopeful now that the Constitutional Court has lifted the HDP treasury aid freeze. However, I still have concerns about the ongoing closure case and the overall unity of the opposition as we approach the elections.

[T026] Erdoğan Announces Pension Increase to 7,500 Lira — Pre-Election Largesse: I'm feeling quite frustrated about the government's pension increase, which feels like a desperate attempt to buy votes rather than genuinely address the economic struggles we face. The gap between pensions and inflation is concerning, and it makes me question the sincerity of these measures.

[T027] HDP/YSP Formally Endorses Kılıçdaroğlu — Kurdish Vote Behind Opposition: I'm feeling uneasy about the HDP's endorsement of Kılıçdaroğlu. While it may energize Kurdish voters, it also raises concerns about how this will be perceived by nationalist critics and the potential backlash it could bring. I hope for a united opposition, but the tensions are palpable.

[T028] Erdoğan Raises Civil Servant Salaries 45% — Five Days Before Election: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with Erdoğan's last-minute salary raise, which seems like a desperate attempt to buy votes. The economic situation is dire, and this won't solve the real issues we face. I hope for a change, but the political landscape is complex and worrisome.

[T029] CAND-A: Muharrem İnce Withdraws — Citing Smear Campaign / Deepfake Video: I'm deeply troubled by İnce's withdrawal and the smear campaign against him. It feels like the political landscape is becoming increasingly toxic, and I'm worried about the implications for our democracy.

[T030A_first_round_vote_decision] First-round presidential vote decision: I feel a mix of frustration and hope as I prepare to vote. The political landscape is troubling, especially with İnce's withdrawal and the smear campaigns, but I still believe in the potential for change with Kılıçdaroğlu.

[T030B_first_round_result_revealed] ROUND1: First Round — Erdoğan 49.52%, Kılıçdaroğlu 44.88%, Oğan 5.17%: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and hope after the first round results. While Kılıçdaroğlu didn't perform as well as I had hoped, I still believe in the possibility of change in the runoff.

[T031] RUN-A: Kılıçdaroğlu's Nationalist Turn — Anti-Refugee Rhetoric Intensifies: I'm feeling conflicted about Kılıçdaroğlu's shift toward anti-refugee rhetoric. While I understand the need to appeal to nationalist sentiments, it worries me that this may alienate other important voter bases. I still hope for a better future, but the political landscape is increasingly troubling.

[T032] RUN-B: Sinan Oğan Endorses Erdoğan: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the political landscape, especially after Oğan's endorsement of Erdoğan. Kılıçdaroğlu's nationalist shift worries me, but I still hope for a better future.

[T033] RUN-C: Kılıçdaroğlu Signs Özdağ Protocol — '13 Million Refugees' Pledge: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and concern about Kılıçdaroğlu's recent nationalist shift and the Özdağ Protocol. While I still hope for change, the political landscape is becoming increasingly troubling.

[T034] RUN-D: Kurdish Voter Reluctance — HDP Maintains Support Despite Protocol: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and concern about the political landscape, especially with Kılıçdaroğlu's nationalist shift and the Özdağ Protocol. I still hope for change, but the situation is increasingly troubling.

[T035A_runoff_vote_decision] Runoff presidential vote decision: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and hope as I consider my decision for the runoff. Kılıçdaroğlu's recent nationalist rhetoric is concerning, but I still believe he offers a better alternative to Erdoğan.

[T035B_final_result_revealed] FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82%: I'm feeling a deep sense of disappointment after the runoff results. Despite my hopes for change with Kılıçdaroğlu, it seems the nationalist rhetoric has alienated many, including myself. The political landscape feels more divided than ever, and I'm left wondering what the future holds for our country.
